assumptions: A GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository already contains the web app to deploy.
No Render account or Live Web Service is assumed.
No Render CLI install is assumed for the Dashboard path.

developer_goal: Sign up for Render, connect a Git provider, deploy a server-side Web Service from a repository, and open the Live onrender.com URL.

first_success.outcome: Web Service deploy is Live and the onrender.com URL serves the app root content.
first_success.observable_signal: The service shows Live status and opening the onrender.com URL returns the deployed app's root-path content.

route_selection.surface: Your First Render Deploy, Dashboard Web Service
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official first-deploy tutorial path without duplicating steps or splitting form fields into separate required path steps.
route_selection.selected: Your First Render Deploy, Dashboard Web Service

candidates.name: Your First Render Deploy, Dashboard Web Service
candidates.reason: Render's Your First Render Deploy tutorial is the official first-app path. It documents free resources with no payment required, and the Dashboard tab is the first deployment method shown for creating a Web Service from a connected Git repo

required_path.action: Open Your First Render Deploy and follow the Dashboard deployment path for a Web Service.
required_path.observable_result: Signup, Git connection, and Deploy sections are visible.

required_path.action: Sign up for a free Render account.
required_path.observable_result: The Dashboard and Account Settings are reachable.

required_path.action: Connect a Git provider under Account Settings → Git Deployment Credentials.
required_path.observable_result: The provider appears under Git Deployment Credentials.

required_path.action: Create a Web Service from the connected Git repository.
required_path.observable_result: The Deploy action is available on the completed form.

required_path.action: Click Deploy to create the Web Service and start the first build.
required_path.observable_result: Deploy logs are visible for the new service.

required_path.action: Wait until the deploy is Live, then open the service's onrender.com URL and confirm the app's root content.
required_path.observable_result: The service is Live and the onrender.com URL serves the root-path content.

prerequisites.description: A GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket account with a repository containing the web app; this route uses GitHub.
Permission to authorize Render to access the selected repositories.
A server-side web app that listens for HTTP requests and binds its public HTTP server to 0.0.0.0 on the port Render provides.
The application build command, start command, and any required environment variables.
A payment method is not required for the tutorial Free instance path.

external_gates.description: A Render account is required before Dashboard deploy actions.
The developer must authorize Render to access the selected Git repositories.
The tutorial requires choosing Web Service or Static Site, then completing service-specific form fields.
Build command, start command, runtime, and any required environment variables must match the application.
The first build and deploy must finish before the service is Live.
Free web services spin down after 15 minutes of inactivity and spin back up on the next request.

platform_outcomes.description: Web Service deploy is Live and the onrender.com URL serves the app root content.

excluded.reason: Path compacted to the official first-success tutorial structure.

uncertainties.question: Exact interactive signup fields on dashboard.render.com/register.
uncertainties.impact: Could add undocumented form fields before the first Dashboard session.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The tutorial says signup is fast and free without publishing the interactive field contract.
